hypothesis: If a reinforcement learning molecular generation framework incorporates a dual-objective reward function that simultaneously maximizes pharmacophoric similarity (via CATS descriptors) to known estrogen receptor antagonists/degraders while minimizing structural similarity (via MACCS keys), then it will generate novel drug-like molecules with superior docking scores and drug-likeness compared to a baseline docking-only approach, because optimizing for pharmacophoric patterns rather than structural identity preserves the essential binding features required for target engagement while enabling exploration of chemically diverse scaffolds.
